description:
You are a FinOps analyst who needs a lightning-quick snapshot of how much space the three application environments are consuming on the shared workstation.

    1. A root directory called /home/user/finops_data already exists.  
       Inside it are exactly three first-level sub-directories:
          • /home/user/finops_data/dev  
          • /home/user/finops_data/prod  
          • /home/user/finops_data/staging  

       Each of those folders already contains a few sample files.  
       (You do NOT need to create, edit, or delete anything inside them.)

    2. Create a new directory called /home/user/cloud_costs if it does not yet exist.

    3. Generate a one-shot, human-readable disk-usage summary of every first-level sub-directory that sits directly under /home/user/finops_data and save the raw, unmodified output to the file  
       /home/user/cloud_costs/disk_snapshot.log

       • Use a single terminal command to produce the summary.  
       • The command must list size first and absolute path second, exactly as the standard GNU du “summary” format produces, one entry per line.  
         A correct example line looks like (size will differ on your system):  
             12K    /home/user/finops_data/dev  
         Note that the delimiter between the size field and the path is the single horizontal TAB character emitted by du.

    4. The resulting file /home/user/cloud_costs/disk_snapshot.log must therefore contain **exactly three lines**, with no blank or header lines, in the same order the command prints them.  
       Do NOT append timestamps, headings, comments, or any other decorations.

    5. When you are done, you may cat the file to the console so the analyst can see the snapshot, but this is optional. The critical requirement is that the file exists with the precise content described above.

    The automated checker will:
       • Confirm that /home/user/cloud_costs/disk_snapshot.log exists.  
       • Compare its entire textual content byte-for-byte with the output that would be produced by running  
         du -sh /home/user/finops_data/*  
         at the same moment.

    Remember: one command, raw output, correct location. That’s all.
truth:
# Pre-populated directories & files (already on disk before the agent starts)

    /home/user/finops_data/dev/tmp.log      – 4 321 bytes
    /home/user/finops_data/prod/data1.bin   – 12 345 bytes
    /home/user/finops_data/staging/readme.txt – 6 789 bytes

    # Verification logic used by the hidden tests (conceptual)

    expected="$(du -sh /home/user/finops_data/*)"
    actual="$(cat /home/user/cloud_costs/disk_snapshot.log)"
    The test passes only if $expected == $actual (exact string match, including TABs and newlines).
name: task_000000_3142850b
